title: Thelypodium brachycarpum
taxonomy: Thelypodium brachycarpum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is a member of the genus Thelypodium, which is primarily found in North America. Thelypodium brachycarpum is a perennial plant that produces small white or yellow flowers and grows in dry to moist areas. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is often found growing in open areas, such as meadows and grasslands.
